mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or negative mean returns following rate cuts across a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which forces equity valuations lower over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer discretionary spending and residential investment, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a positive mean 60-day forward return (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index during periods of high inflation reflects supply-side bottlenecks and margin compression, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high inflation periods yield positive 5-day forward returns in over 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate combined with a declining consumer price index signals a disinflationary easing cycle that improves real corporate profits and equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL produces negative or statistically insignificant 20-day forward returns across multiple easing cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls indicate weakening economic activity and reduced aggregate demand, which systematically depresses corporate earnings expectations and S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the rank correlation remains below 0.02 over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms robust economic expansion and strong corporate revenue fundamentals, which immediately lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses the 2.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment during a post-recession recovery phase signals accelerating capacity utilization and labor market tightening, which drives equity risk premium compression and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of rising INDPRO and falling UNRATE in recovery phases fails to generate positive 20-day forward returns in at least 60% of historical recovery periods.
